title: anisodens arvensis
intro: Anisodens arvensis, also known as Wild Celery or Queen Anne's Lace, is a biennial plant belonging to the parsley family (Apiaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antispasmodic and expectorant abilities, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is rich in vitamins and minerals, providing several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion. Anisodens arvensis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile herbal remedy.
